-- This file contains the database table structure for SkillHub.


-- ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- TABLE: users
-- Stores every registered account on the platform.
--
-- Columns:
--   user_id       → auto-generated unique ID for each user
--   full_name     → user's display name (shown in navbar, profile)
--   email         → login email — must be unique across all users
--   password_hash → bcrypt hash from PHP's password_hash()
--                   NEVER store plain-text passwords here
--   role          → 'user' = regular student, 'admin' = site admin
--                   Admin gets access to the dashboard; user cannot
--   profile_image → relative path to uploaded profile picture
--                   e.g. uploads/profile_pictures/abc123.jpg
--                   NULL if no picture has been uploaded
--   created_at    → when the account was created (auto-set)
--   updated_at    → auto-updates whenever the row is changed
-- ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CREATE TABLE users (
  user_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
  full_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  email VARCHAR(150) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  password_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  role ENUM('user', 'admin') NOT NULL DEFAULT 'user',
  profile_image VARCHAR(255) DEFAULT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);



-- ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- TABLE: categories
-- Workshop categories shown in the filter dropdown on Services
-- and in the Add/Edit workshop dropdown in the admin dashboard.
--
-- Columns:
--   category_id   → auto-generated unique ID
--   category_name → display name e.g. 'Web Development', 'AI Tools'
--                   UNIQUE prevents duplicate category names
-- ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CREATE TABLE categories (
    category_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    category_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL UNIQUE
);



-- ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- TABLE: workshops
-- Each row is one workshop session available for booking.
-- Shown on the Services page and the Schedule page.
--
-- Columns:
--   workshop_id     → auto-generated unique ID
--   title           → workshop name e.g. 'Python for Beginners'
--   description     → what students will learn in this session
--   category_id     → links to categories table (FOREIGN KEY)
--                     must match an existing category
--   instructor_id   → links to instructors table (FOREIGN KEY)
--                     NULL if no instructor has been assigned yet
--   workshop_date   → date the workshop takes place (YYYY-MM-DD)
--   start_time      → time the workshop starts (HH:MM:SS)
--   end_time        → time the workshop ends — must be after start
--   available_seats → seats left for booking; decreases by 1 each
--                     time a student books; never goes below 0
--   image_path      → optional path to a workshop image file
--                     NULL shows a placeholder card instead
--   created_at      → when the workshop was added
--   updated_at      → auto-updates when the workshop is edited
-- ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CREATE TABLE workshops (
    workshop_id      INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    title            VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,
    description      TEXT         NOT NULL,
    category_id      INT          NOT NULL,
    instructor_id    INT          DEFAULT NULL,
    workshop_date    DATE         NOT NULL,
    start_time       TIME         NOT NULL,
    end_time         TIME         NOT NULL,
    available_seats  INT          NOT NULL DEFAULT 30,
    image_path       VARCHAR(255) DEFAULT NULL,
    created_at       TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    updated_at       TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,

    -- If a category is deleted, block the delete to protect workshops
    FOREIGN KEY (category_id)  REFERENCES categories(category_id),
    -- If an instructor is deleted, set their workshops to unassigned
    FOREIGN KEY (instructor_id) REFERENCES instructors(instructor_id) ON DELETE SET NULL

    CONSTRAINT fk_workshops_category
        FOREIGN KEY (category_id)
        REFERENCES categories(category_id)
        ON UPDATE CASCADE
        ON DELETE RESTRICT
);



-- ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- TABLE: bookings
-- Each row is one workshop reservation made by a student.
-- Shown in the student's profile under "My Bookings".
--
-- Columns:
--   booking_id  → auto-generated unique ID
--   workshop_id → which workshop was booked (FOREIGN KEY)
--                 ON DELETE CASCADE: if workshop is deleted,
--                 all its bookings are automatically removed
--   first_name  → student's first name at time of booking
--   last_name   → student's last name (optional)
--   email       → student's email — used to identify the user
--                 and to prevent double-booking the same workshop
--   created_at  → when the booking was made
-- ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CREATE TABLE bookings (
    booking_id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INT NULL,
    workshop_id INT NOT NULL,
    first_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    last_name VARCHAR(100) NULL,
    email VARCHAR(150) NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,

    CONSTRAINT fk_bookings_user
        FOREIGN KEY (user_id)
        REFERENCES users(user_id)
        ON UPDATE CASCADE
        ON DELETE SET NULL,

    CONSTRAINT fk_bookings_workshop
        FOREIGN KEY (workshop_id)
        REFERENCES workshops(workshop_id)
        ON UPDATE CASCADE
        ON DELETE CASCADE,

    CONSTRAINT uq_booking_email_workshop
        UNIQUE (email, workshop_id)
);



-- ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- TABLE: instructors
-- People who lead workshops. Admin can add/edit instructors
-- from the dashboard and assign them to workshops.
--
-- Columns:
--   instructor_id → auto-generated unique ID
--   full_name     → instructor's full name e.g. 'Ahmed Hassan'
--   title         → honorific e.g. 'Dr.', 'Prof.', 'Mr.'
--                   NULL if no title
--   email         → instructor's contact email (optional)
--   created_at    → when the instructor record was added
-- ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS instructors (
    instructor_id  INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    full_name      VARCHAR(150) NOT NULL,
    title          VARCHAR(100) DEFAULT NULL,
    email          VARCHAR(150) DEFAULT NULL,
    created_at     TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);
-- Seed instructors — INSERT IGNORE skips duplicates
INSERT IGNORE INTO instructors (instructor_id, full_name, title, email) VALUES
    (1, 'Ahmed Hassan', 'Dr.',   'a.hassan@skillhub.edu'),
    (2, 'Lina Torres',  'Prof.', 'l.torres@skillhub.edu'),
    (3, 'Sarah Kim',    'Dr.',   's.kim@skillhub.edu'),
    (4, 'James Park',   'Mr.',   'j.park@skillhub.edu');

INSERT INTO instructors (title, full_name, email)
VALUES ('Dr.', 'Omar Al-Farsi', 'o.alfarsi@skillhub.edu');

-- ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- TABLE: feedback
-- Stores feedback submitted by students via the Feedback page.
-- Admin can view all submissions on the dashboard, reply to them,
-- and mark them as resolved.
--
-- Columns:
--   feedback_id          → auto-generated unique ID
--   user_id              → links to the user who submitted it
--                          ON DELETE SET NULL: keeps feedback even
--                          if the user account is later deleted
--   name                 → student's name at time of submission
--   email                → student's email
--   rating               → overall rating: Good / Average / Poor
--   workshops_interested → comma-separated workshop interests
--                          e.g. 'web-dev, ai-tools'
--   preferred_time       → preferred session time e.g. 'morning'
--   comments             → open-ended feedback text from student
--   admin_reply          → the most recent admin reply (quick-view)
--                          full history is in feedback_messages table
--   resolved             → 0 = active, 1 = resolved (hidden from view)
--                          resolved feedback stays in DB for reference
--   submitted_at         → when the student submitted the form
-- ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS feedback (
    feedback_id           INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id               INT           DEFAULT NULL,
    name                  VARCHAR(100)  NOT NULL,
    email                 VARCHAR(150)  NOT NULL,
    rating                ENUM('Good', 'Average', 'Poor') NOT NULL,
    workshops_interested  VARCHAR(255)  DEFAULT NULL,
    preferred_time        VARCHAR(50)   DEFAULT NULL,
    comments              TEXT          DEFAULT NULL,
    admin_reply           TEXT          DEFAULT NULL,
    resolved              TINYINT(1)    NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    submitted_at          TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,

    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(user_id) ON DELETE SET NULL
);



-- ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- TABLE: feedback_messages
-- Stores the full history of admin replies to each feedback item.
-- Unlike feedback.admin_reply (which only keeps the latest),
-- this table stacks all messages so nothing is overwritten.
-- Each time the admin sends or edits a reply, a new row is added.
--
-- Columns:
--   message_id  → auto-generated unique ID
--   feedback_id → which feedback this message belongs to
--                 ON DELETE CASCADE: removed if feedback is deleted
--   sender      → always 'admin' for now (future: could add 'user')
--   message     → the reply text sent by the admin
--   sent_at     → when the message was sent
-- ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS feedback_messages (
    message_id   INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    feedback_id  INT NOT NULL,
    sender       ENUM('admin') NOT NULL DEFAULT 'admin',
    message      TEXT NOT NULL,
    sent_at      TIMESTAMP NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,

    FOREIGN KEY (feedback_id) REFERENCES feedback(feedback_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
);




Admin query: 
INSERT INTO users (full_name, email, password_hash, role)
VALUES (
  'Admin',
  'admin_skilhub@outlook.sa',
  'cbhw249s@mdkA',
  'admin'
);








-- Admin account should be created manually later using a hashed password.
-- Do not store plain-text passwords.

-- Add replies_seen_at to users table
-- Stores when user last opened the My Replies tab
-- Used to determine which admin replies are unread
ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN replies_seen_at DATETIME DEFAULT NULL;



===
Run these SQL commands in phpMyAdmin inside `skillhub_db`:

```sql
ALTER TABLE workshops
ADD instructor_specialty VARCHAR(100) DEFAULT NULL,
ADD instructor_experience VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT NULL,
ADD instructor_bio TEXT DEFAULT NULL;
```

Then run:

```sql
UPDATE workshops
SET 
  instructor_specialty = CASE instructor
    WHEN 'Dr. Ahmed Hassan' THEN 'Data Science'
    WHEN 'Prof. Lina Torres' THEN 'Project Management'
    WHEN 'Ms. Sara Ali' THEN 'Graphic Design'
    WHEN 'Mr. Omar Khalid' THEN 'Mobile App Development'
    WHEN 'Ms. Nora Saeed' THEN 'Cybersecurity'
    WHEN 'Ms. Huda Salem' THEN 'Communication Skills'
    WHEN 'Mr. Faisal Nasser' THEN 'Business Analytics'
    WHEN 'Ms. Reem Ali' THEN 'UI/UX Design'
    WHEN 'Mr. Khalid Omar' THEN 'Web Development'
    WHEN 'Ms. Dana Fahad' THEN 'Digital Marketing'
    ELSE instructor_specialty
  END,

  instructor_experience = CASE instructor
    WHEN 'Dr. Ahmed Hassan' THEN '8 Years'
    WHEN 'Prof. Lina Torres' THEN '10 Years'
    WHEN 'Ms. Sara Ali' THEN '5 Years'
    WHEN 'Mr. Omar Khalid' THEN '6 Years'
    WHEN 'Ms. Nora Saeed' THEN '5 Years'
    WHEN 'Ms. Huda Salem' THEN '4 Years'
    WHEN 'Mr. Faisal Nasser' THEN '7 Years'
    WHEN 'Ms. Reem Ali' THEN '6 Years'
    WHEN 'Mr. Khalid Omar' THEN '7 Years'
    WHEN 'Ms. Dana Fahad' THEN '5 Years'
    ELSE instructor_experience
  END,

  instructor_bio = CASE instructor
    WHEN 'Dr. Ahmed Hassan' THEN 'Experienced instructor in data analysis, Python, and practical machine learning concepts.'
    WHEN 'Prof. Lina Torres' THEN 'Project management specialist with experience in planning, teamwork, and agile practices.'
    WHEN 'Ms. Sara Ali' THEN 'Creative design trainer focused on visual communication, branding, and beginner-friendly design tools.'
    WHEN 'Mr. Omar Khalid' THEN 'Mobile development instructor experienced in building user-friendly Android and iOS app concepts.'
    WHEN 'Ms. Nora Saeed' THEN 'Certified trainer specialized in security awareness and account protection.'
    WHEN 'Ms. Huda Salem' THEN 'Communication skills trainer focused on presentations, teamwork, and professional confidence.'
    WHEN 'Mr. Faisal Nasser' THEN 'Business analytics trainer experienced in dashboards, reporting, and data-driven decision making.'
    WHEN 'Ms. Reem Ali' THEN 'UI/UX designer focused on user research, wireframes, and clean interface design.'
    WHEN 'Mr. Khalid Omar' THEN 'Full-stack developer experienced in HTML, CSS, JavaScript, PHP, and MySQL.'
    WHEN 'Ms. Dana Fahad' THEN 'Digital marketing trainer focused on content strategy, social media, and campaign basics.'
    ELSE instructor_bio
  END;
```
